title: OpenAI Fixes 18-Year-Old GNU libunwind Bug by Treating Crash Debugging Like Epidemiology
summary: OpenAI found two unrelated bugs masquerading as one in ChatGPT's data infrastructure. Silent hardware corruption on one Azure host and an 18-year-old race condition in GNU libunwind's setcontext function with a one-instruction vulnerability window. The breakthrough came from switching to population-level crash analysis rather than examining individual core dumps. By Steef-Jan Wiggers
title_cn: OpenAI 通过像流行病学一样对待崩溃调试来修复 18 年之久的 GNU libunwind Bug
summary_cn: OpenAI 在 ChatGPT 的数据基础设施中发现了两个不相关的错误，它们伪装成一个错误。一台 Azure 主机上的静默硬件损坏以及 GNU libunwind 的 setcontext 函数中存在 18 年历史的竞争条件

title: Article: Trade-Offs in Multi-Region Architectures: Latency vs. Cost
summary: Adding cloud regions changes latency and cost in ways simple math can't capture. This article presents a framework from multiple launches: decompose your latency budget before committing to infrastructure, choose deployment patterns by consistency and traffic profile, and optimize before expanding. A phased approach cut latency 35% through routing alone, before a new region brought it under 60ms. By Uttara Asthana
